sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: FC København have shown resilience in their last 5 matches, remaining unbeaten in 4 with 2 wins and 2 draws, scoring 10 goals but conceding 9. Napoli, on the other hand, are also in strong form, unbeaten in their last 8 games, with 2 wins and 3 draws in their last 5, scoring 7 goals while conceding 4. Both teams display attacking intent but have defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: FC København have scored 10 goals this season but conceded 16, ranking poorly in defensive metrics (32nd in least goals conceded). Napoli mirror these stats with 6 goals scored and 11 conceded, also struggling defensively (24th in standings). København's high conversion rate of big chances (53.3%, 2nd in league) could be a key factor at home.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: There is no recent head-to-head data available for the last two encounters between FC København and Napoli. This adds an element of unpredictability to the matchup, making form and home advantage critical factors.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Tactics
sections.text: For København, leveraging their aerial strength (8th in aerial duels won) and late-game scoring (43.75% of goals after 76 minutes) could be vital. Napoli's Scott McTominay (7.58 Sofascore rating, 3 goals in last 5) is a standout threat, with their balanced possession play (65% in recent games) likely to test København's low possession stats (44%, 32nd in league).

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore's prediction of a 51% chance for a home win, combined with København's unbeaten streak and Napoli's struggles on the road, the Danish side holds a slight edge. However, Napoli's unbeaten run and ability to score late (27.27% of goals after 76 minutes) suggest they can snatch a result. Expect a tight, high-scoring affair at Parken Stadium.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Napoli lead FC København 1-0 at Parken Stadium in Copenhagen. The away side has dominated possession and created more threatening opportunities, with Scott McTominay heading home the opener in the 39th minute. FC København, on the other hand, have struggled to create chances and are further hampered by a red card to Thomas Delaney, leaving them a man down for the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Napoli have controlled the game with significant ball possession and numerous entries into the final third and penalty area. They've capitalized on their dominance with a well-taken goal and have shown composure in maintaining their lead. FC København, despite showing some resilience in duels, have failed to register any shots on target or create notable chances. The red card has severely impacted their ability to compete on even terms, putting them on the back foot for the remainder of the match.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: Throughout the first half, Napoli have consistently shown greater attacking intent with frequent spells of pressure in the opposition's half. Their momentum peaked around the time of their goal, showcasing a sustained push that overwhelmed København's defense. In contrast, FC København had very few moments of attacking threat, with only brief periods where they managed to push forward, often quickly losing momentum to Napoli's control.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player performances
halftimeSections.text: For Napoli, Scott McTominay has been the standout performer, not only scoring the decisive goal but also contributing significantly in midfield with key passes and interceptions. Stanislav Lobotka has also been instrumental in dictating play from the center of the park. On the FC København side, the red card to Thomas Delaney has been a major blow, disrupting their midfield structure. Defenders like Junnosuke Suzuki have tried to hold the line with multiple clearances, but the lack of offensive output from players like Andreas Cornelius and Elias Achouri has been evident.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: With a numerical advantage, Napoli are likely to continue dominating possession and look to exploit spaces left by København's reduced numbers. They may focus on maintaining control while seeking to add to their lead through patient build-up play. FC København will need to adopt a defensive mindset, possibly sitting deep to protect against further damage while hoping for a rare counter-attack opportunity. Their lack of manpower could severely limit any comeback potential, making damage control their primary objective.
